works.author: Paulína Abrahámová
works.title: Cocoon chair
works.text:
ím. Cieľom bolo vytvoriť exteriérové sedenie s funkciou odievania sa pre dlhší pobyt na vzduchu. Téma space to space sa tu odohráva ako vytváranie si vlastného “spacu“ zahalením sa do nábytku. Integrovaná vesta obopína kreslo a vytvára pocit tepla, ochrany a komfortu v chladnejších mesiacoch. Vestu vieme využiť ako samostatný módny kúsok odzipsovaním od základnej formy sedenia. Sedenie bez vesty vytvára pútavé čalúnenie evokujúce povrch outdoorového zimného oblečenia. Nepremokavý materiál umožňuje používanie v exteriéri počas chladnejších večerov, vytvára unikátne sedenie na vianočné trhy či na terasy horských chát a hotelov.

ANGLICKÁ VERZIA:

Cocoon chair combines ergonomic seating with clothing. The aim was to create outdoor seating with the function of dressing for longer stays in the air. The Space to space theme is played out here as creating your own “space” by dress yourself in furniture. The integrated vest surrounds the chair and creates a feeling of warmth, protection and comfort in the colder months. The vest can be used as a separate fashion piece by unzipping it from the basic form of seating. Sitting without the vest creates an eye-catching upholstery evoking the surface of outdoor winter clothing. The waterproof material allows for outdoor use during colder evenings, creating unique seating for Christmas markets or on the terraces of mountain huts and hotels.

works.author: Martina Ďalogová
works.title: Glitch
works.text: The project interprets the theme Space to Space as a generational shift in the way the world is perceived. Glitch, originally a digital phenomenon, is transferred into a physical object and functions as a visual metaphor for the difference between a generation raised in an analog environment and Generation Z shaped by the digital world. This generational shift highlights the moment in which old and new readings of reality no longer align.

works.author: Hana Šegedová
works.title: SPOJOVÁ school garden
works.text: The school garden was created as a connection between the school's identity and the world of children's imagination. It responds to the environment, the diversity of ages, creates a space for cooperation and quiet discovery. It is a place where children learn through touch, movement and play. The natural materials support authentic contact with the outside world and allow the garden to adapt to every season.

works.author: Hana Šegedová
works.title: UMAMA
works.text: UMAMA is a piece of furniture inspired by the balance, strength, and ingenuity of African women and mothers. It reflects their ability to adapt, take responsibility, and turn everyday tasks into thoughtful, functional acts. It combines the tradition of carrying loads on the head with modern life, showing that heritage can naturally function today in the wider context of space.

works.author: Hana Šegedová
works.title: WARKA house
works.text: The design combines the principle of the Warka Tower with a modern interpretation of traditional African housing to address the shortage of drinking water. Water collection is integrated into the roofs, creating a single functional unit with the architecture and interior. The project uses local materials, respects local lifestyles, and aims to sustainably improve daily living conditions.

works.author: Ema Kmecová
works.title: Face to Face
works.text:
Name: Face to „Face“ 

Autor: Ema Kmecová

Supervisor: Mgr. art. Lucia Spišiaková Kružlicová, ArtD.



Anotácia: 

Stolička Face to „Face“ je bodom, kde sa fyzická prítomnosť stretáva s neviditeľnou sieťou digitálneho sveta a vzdialenosť sa premieňa na spojenie. Jej konštrukcia pripomína káblové vedenia a toky informácií, zatiaľ čo polykarbonát odhaľuje vnútorný svet, ako pohľad do živého systému, ktorým prechádzajú dáta aj ľudské spojenia. Stáva sa tak mostom medzi človekom, priestorom a technológiou, medzi hmotným a tým, čo existuje len ako prenos, signál a prítomnosť na diaľku.

Annotation: 

The Face to „Face“ chair is a point where physical presence meets the invisible network of the digital world, transforming distance into connection. Its structure evokes cable pathways and flows of information, while the polycarbonate reveals the inner world, a glimpse into a living system through which data and human connections pass. It becomes a bridge between people, space, and technology, between the tangible and that which exists only as transmission, signal and presence at a distance.
warnings: V annotations nalezeno 2 souborů, žádný s EN markerem. Použit první soubor.
Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Gréta Brajerová
works.title: Errans
works.text:
errans

autor: Gréta Brajerová

supervisor: Mgr. art. Lucia Spišiaková Kružlicová, ArtD.





Projekt errans (z lat. blúdiaci) je sociálno-ekologický projekt v podchode, ktorý navracia dôstojnosť ľuďom bez domova a znehodnoteným nákupným vozíkom. Funguje ako chránená dielňa: človek v núdzi si prácou na redizajne vozíkov odpracuje nocľah. Prechádza cez štyri zóny: ošetrenie, očista s reflexiou v zrkadlách, spánok vo vlastnej posteli a presklená dielňa, spájajú pomoc s prácou. Errans dáva viditeľnosť a priestor tým, ktorým bol dlho odopieraný.





The Errans project (from the Latin for 'wandering') is a socio-ecological initiative located in an underpass that restores dignity to both homeless people and discarded shopping carts. Operating as a sheltered workshop, it allows individuals in need to earn a night’s stay by working on the redesign of these carts. The process moves through four distinct zones: treatment, purification (with reflection in mirrors), sleep in a private bed, and a glass-walled workshop. By combining social aid with labor, Errans provides visibility and a dedicated space to those who have long been overlooked.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Ema Kováčová
works.title: DUENDE
works.text:
Názov: DUENDEAutor: Ema KováčováSupervisor: doc. Mgr. art. Marián Ihring, ArtD.



Anotácia:

Duende je zhmotnené napätie - bod, kde sa ticho mení na vášeň a pokoj na pohyb. Objekt nesie surovú reč flamenca: plynulosť ramien bracea a dravú dynamiku zapatea tesne pred výbuchom.Ostré uhly sa tu stretávajú s jemným nádychom v dialógu mužského a ženského princípu. Nie je len sedením, ale vizuálnou stopou emócie, ktorá sa rodí hlboko vo vnútri a cez gesto nadobúda hmotnú podobu.



Annotation:

Duende is embodied tension - a point where silence transforms into passion and stillness into movement. The object carries the raw language of flamenco: the fluidity of the shoulders in braceo and the fierce dynamics of zapateado just before the explosion. Sharp angles meet a gentle breath in the dialogue of masculine and feminine principles. It is not merely a seat, but a visual trace of emotion, born deep within and taking shape through gesture.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martina Hatalová
works.title: From Air to Ground
works.text:
í pristane vo vtáčej búdke, kde si dobije baterky predtým, než znovu vzlietne.

ENWhat if, after placing an order, a package fell straight from the sky? What if it were delivered to its “nest” by a battery-powered carrier pigeon? I wait impatiently for it‘s arrival. Above my head, I hear the buzz of a drone aiming precisely at a circular symbol where it can safely place the package. After delivery, it lands in a birdhouse to recharge before flying again.

works.author: Samuel Rusnák
works.title: YTERA
warnings: Chybí textový obsah.

works.author: Viola Muchová
works.title: Layra
works.text: The Layra object explores the concept of space as a changing relationship between the body, material, and form. Within the Space to Space context, it creates a dynamic space through the physical interaction of the user with layered textile material that responds through movement and deformation. The seating thus becomes an active element that transforms its form and visual character, creating a new spatial experience between the object and the human body.

works.author: Alexandre Guezenoc
works.title: How
works.text:
SPACE_TO_SPACE (2026). The poster series interprets SPACE TO SPACE as a relationship between physical and mental space. Intense colors, irony, and metaphors of transition confront cosmic expansion with questions of responsibility, consumerism, and freedom of choice. The works ask whether, without a shift in perspective, we are capable of designing empathetic and ethical futures.



PL:

SPACE_TO_SPACE (2026). Cykl plakatów interpretuje SPACE TO SPACE jako relację między przestrzenią fizyczną i mentalną. Intensywna kolorystyka, ironia i metafory przejścia zestawiają ekspansję w kosmos z odpowiedzialnością, konsumpcją i wolnością wyboru. Prace pytają, czy bez zmiany myślenia potrafimy projektować empatyczne i etyczne przyszłości.
warnings: V annotations nalezeno 2 souborů, žádný s EN markerem. Použit první soubor.

works.author: Michał Sieczka
works.title: Matches
works.text: Matches(2025). This experimental documentary, shot on 16mm film, explores tree felling as a disruption of local memory and the landscape’s natural rhythm. The raw materiality of the analogue medium, with its grain and fragility, serves as a parallel narrative layer to the physical changes depicted on screen.
warnings: V annotations nalezeno 3 souborů, žádný s EN markerem. Použit první soubor.

works.author: Jaka Kordiš, Matic Lesjak, Črt Štrubelj
works.title: What remains
works.text: What Remains? is a speculative design project that questions how we treat death. What if the body itself became the vessel? Using blood and hair, it proposes a biodegradable urn, formed through dehydration, coagulation, and compression, that returns the deceased to earth, dissolving quietly back into the cycle of life.

works.text: SEAPACK is a sustainable material developed from dried seagrass gathered on

works.author: Kuan-Yu Chen, Guan-Bo Huang, Hsuan-Wu Sung, An-Chieh Cheng
works.title: Ning-Stones
works.text:
Ning Stones enhances sensory perception and alleviates users’ everyday stress. Crafted from recycled marine glass, this design offers a multi-sensory escape via a magnetic levitation lighting, wave drums, floating oil stones, massage stones, and aromatherapy stones. Merging sustainable materials with interactive functions, it harmonizes soul and space, transforming waste into a serene, high-quality meditation experience.



寧石放大感官體驗，舒緩使用者的日常壓力。以回收海廢玻璃精心打造，本設計結合磁浮燈光、波浪鼓、浮油石、按摩石與擴香石，帶來多重感官交織的沉浸式放鬆體驗。透過永續材料與互動功能的融合，調和心靈與空間，將廢棄物轉化為寧靜且高品質的冥想體驗。

works.author: Jozef Mikušinec
works.title: Space Simulation
works.text:
Space Simulation

Jozef Mikušinec



Space Simulation simuluje objekty ve vesmíru, které ovlivňují dráhu světla a vytvářejí vizuální deformace zvoleného pozadí – v tomto případě fotografie Marsu. Ačkoliv objekty existují na 2D ploše (bez skutečného třetího rozměru), vzájemně na sebe působí gravitačně podle klasických fyzikálních zákonů, přitahují se jako skutečná nebeská tělesa. Světlo se kolem těchto objektů ohýbá způsobem podobným gravitačnímu čočkování (gravitational lensing), ale s použitím samostatného nastavitelného parametru – konstanty ohybu světla (L), která umožňuje jemně regulovat efekt deformace nezávisle na gravitačních interakcích. Výsledkem je vizuálně působivá simulace, kde vzájemná interakce hmoty a světla vytváří dynamický, neustále se měnící obraz, ilustrující jemný, přesto silný vliv gravitace na světlo.



Space Simulation simulates objects in space that influence the path of light, producing visual distortions of a chosen background image—in this case, a photograph of Mars. Although the objects exist on a 2D plane (without a true third dimension), they interact gravitationally according to classical physics, attracting one another as real celestial bodies would. Light is bent around these objects in a manner analogous to gravitational lensing, but with a separate adjustable parameter—the light-bending constant (L)—allowing for fine control of the distortion effect independent of the gravitational interactions. The result is a visually striking simulation where the interplay of mass and light creates a dynamic, ever-changing image, illustrating the subtle yet powerful influence of gravity on light.



Projekt může být prezentovaný buď jako video, obrázky.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Adam Svoboda
works.title: Nebula
works.text:
é pomocí geometry nodes a inspirované vizuálním jazykem rané vektorové grafiky – ostrým, minimalistickým a lehce retro. Více vrstev animovaného šumu a postupně se měnící barevné palety spolu vzájemně interagují, čímž do kompozice vnášejí hloubku, pohyb a variabilitu. Tyto prvky společně zajišťují, že se mlhovina nachází v neustálém toku a žádné dva snímky nejsou totožné, což zdůrazňuje jak nekonečnou povahu vesmíru, tak generativní proces stojící za jeho vznikem.

Nebula - an endless nebula generated entirely within Blender’s shader editor forms the core of this piece, unfolding as a continuously evolving cosmic environment. The scene is populated by procedurally generated asteroids, created using geometry nodes and inspired by the visual language of early vector graphics—sharp, minimal, and slightly retro. Multiple layers of animated noise and gradually shifting colour palettes interact across the composition, introducing depth, motion, and variation. Together, these elements ensure that the nebula is in constant flux, with no two frames ever appearing the same, emphasizing both the infinite nature of space and the generative process behind its creation.



Můžeme přerendrovat video nebo obrazy dle požadavků.

works.author: Martin Nastoupil
works.title: Resonance in Space
works.text:
ím do třetího rozměru. Namísto statických stop vznikajících na vibrujících deskách se zvuk stává živým, prostorovým jevem. Hrou na klávesnici nebo vytvářením zvuku účastníci aktivují systém, který v reálném čase uvádí částice do pohybu – shlukují se, rozptylují a „tančí“ v závislosti na frekvenci a intenzitě zvukového vstupu. Dílo proměňuje zvuk v hmatatelný vizuální zážitek a zve diváky nejen k pozorování, ale i k aktivní participaci.

Resonance i Space xplores the intersection of acoustics and art, this project reinterprets Chladni’s 18th-century sound patterns by extending them into three-dimensional space. Instead of static traces left by vibrating plates, sound becomes a living, spatial phenomenon. By playing the keyboard or producing sound, participants activate the system, causing particles to respond in real time—gathering, dispersing, and dancing according to the frequencies and intensities of the audio input. The work transforms sound into a tangible visual experience, inviting viewers to both observe and perform.



Projekt může být prezentovaný buď jako video (můžeme připravit delší stopu). Pro interaktivní instalaci je pak třeba:  leap motion, Raspberry Pi, monitor, audio výstup.

works.author: Matúš Talčík
works.title: Darwin’s Cube
works.text:
áší klasickou Hru života Johna Conwaye, což je buněčný automat, do pseudo-3D světa. Hra života se často používá ke zkoumání složitosti, emergentního chování a toho, jak jednoduchá pravidla mohou generovat zdánlivě nepředvídatelné vzorce – elegantní metafora života, evoluce a samo organizace. Místo nekonečné ploché mřížky se hra odehrává na povrchu krychle. Díky „obtočení“ hran pomocí kruhových pravidel se mřížka chová, jako by byla nekonečná, a vytváří tak neustále se vyvíjející vzory, které plynule pokračují přes všechny stěny krychle. Projekt se tak stává meditací o hranicích a nekonečnu – ukazuje, jak jednoduchá pravidla mohou generovat nekonečnou složitost a jak konečné struktury mohou vytvářet iluzi nekonečna.



Darwin’s Cube brings the classic Conway’s Game of Life, a cellular automaton, into a pseudo-3D world. The Game of Life is often used to explore complexity, emergence, and how simple rules can generate seemingly unpredictable behavior—an elegant metaphor for life, evolution, and self-organization. Instead of an endless flat grid, the game unfolds on the surface of a cube. By “wrapping” the edges with circular rules, the grid behaves as if it were infinite, creating continuously evolving patterns that loop seamlessly across the cube’s faces. In doing so, the project becomes a meditation on boundaries and infinity—showing how simple rules can generate endless complexity, and how finite structures can give the illusion of the infinite.



Projekt může být prezentovaný buď jako video, obrázky.

works.author: Patrik Sluka
works.title: Blow Me Away!
works.text: Blow me away! is an interactive visual of a state map with wind trajectories. By blowing into a microphone, the viewer influences the movement of cities and disrupts the stability of the system. The work addresses natural forces, geopolitics, the fragility of borders, and the need for a shift in perspective, where an individual gesture can have a huge impact on society.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Pavol Šeliga
works.title: AS IF NOTHING HAPPENED
works.text:
AS_IF_NOTHING_HAPPENED 

a VR experience

Formal invitation … traversal, world and changes approximately observant of narratives within. It can feel as if nothing is truly changing. It would make you wish. What you see, might not be about pretending at all. So much for waiting. If something mattered, it is there no longer …



Work attempts to frame hard to express abstract feelings and sees the visitor through numerous stages. Experience is made in form of a videogame level Mod for VR game Half-life Alyx.

works.author: Martin Kopera
works.title: Speed of transformation
works.text:
Annotation



The work explores the connection between man and machine as a symbol of the present era, in which speed, performance, and technology have become both a new value and a threat.

The objects are based on the idea that the boundary between the organic and the mechanical is disappearing. At the same time, it depicts the demise and new direction of society.

The aim of the work is to understand speed and technology as a metaphor for today's society: it fascinates, but at the same time destroys the inner balance of the universe.
warnings: V annotations nalezeno 2 souborů, žádný s EN markerem. Použit první soubor.

works.author: Barbora Gajdošová
works.title: Consume
works.text:
ve tvaru vidličky, na svém vrcholu nese onyx. Při přiložení vidličky ke rtům se prázdné místo vyplní onyxem. Sada se zaměřuje na problém nadměrné konzumace v dnešní době. Symbolizuje, jak jsou lidé vedeni k iluzi sebeprázdnoty, kterou potřebují vyplnit materiálními potřebami. Toto prázdno ale není přirozené, ale uměle vytvořené korporacemi. 

The copper lip jewelry has an empty hole in the shape of an onyx. The ring is shaped like a fork, with an onyx sitting at its top. When the fork is placed against the lip, the empty space is filled with the onyx. The set focuses on overconsumption. It symbolizes how people are led to the illusion of emptiness, which they need to fill with material needs. However, this emptiness is not natural, but artificially created by corporations.

works.author: Kateřina Vyroubalová
works.title: Elsewhere
works.text:
Jinde
Kolekce prstenu a přívěsku z mědi a achátů vznikla na téma Space to Space. Inspirací je představa života mimo naši planetu, nikoli však v podobě bytostí podobných člověku. Organismy, které šperky evokují, jsou prostorové struktury připomínající mikroorganismy či energetické formy. Kolekce reaguje na lidskou tendenci chápat neznámé skrze známé vzorce /space/ a otevírá prostor pro svobodnější imaginaci /to space/.
 
Elsewhere
The collection of a ring and a pendant made of copper and agates was created under the theme Space to Space. It draws on the idea of life beyond our planet, not human-like forms. The organisms evoked by the jewellery are spatial structures resembling microorganisms or energy forms. The collection reflects the human tendency to interpret the unknown through familiar patterns /space/ and opens space for freer imagination /to space/.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Michal Grzebinski
works.title: Relic
works.text:
a držen v minimálních bodech, čímž vzniká napětí mezi přírodou a konstrukcí.
This jewelry piece explores the theme of space to space as a transfer from an original space into a new one. The raw stone holds the memory of its place and time of origin, shaped without human intervention. A geometric structure reflects human order and control. The stone is not set harmoniously but forced into place and held at minimal points, creating tension between nature and construction.

works.author: Sára Timošová
works.title: There was one house
warnings: Chybí textový obsah.

works.author: Lena Navrátilová
works.title: Jasmina
works.text:
Jasmina is an isometric journey into the unconscious, gamifying therapy into a 
tangible narrative. Through dreamlike metaphors and evidence-based methods, it 
teaches players to navigate anxiety and depression. This digital sanctuary is more 
than a game - it’s an empathetic tool for personal growth, helping players find clarity 
even in the darkest places.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Lukáš Kučera
works.title: Sinequo
works.text:
Annotation

SINEQUO is a combat-racing game that takes players into the era of the mechanical Renaissance, where horses have been replaced by the first self-propelled steel machines. At the core of the gameplay is a unique mechanic for switching between intense 3D racing and a 2D technical blueprint dimension. In this “planning” mode, the world transforms into a living sketchbook, allowing players to strategically modify their machine mid-race, adapt to track hazards, and strategize.
warnings: V annotations nalezeno 2 souborů, žádný s EN markerem. Použit první soubor.
Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Matouš Marek
works.title: Revenant Earth 2
works.text:
Revenant Earth is a 2D pixel-art strategy-adventure game focused on restoring a planet 
devastated by climate change. As the last human, the player controls weather, soil, 
plant growth, and technology to bring balance back to Earth. The game simulates 
ecosystem processes and promotes understanding of sustainability, environmental 
systems, and the impact of human activity, helping to develop ecological awareness and 
responsible attitudes toward nature.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Jagošová
works.title: Liferaft
works.text:
á část pod hladinou výrazně usnadňuje záchranu tonoucího.



EN

The rescue raft project addresses water safety by reducing response time and improving the accessibility of assistance in emergency situations. Its modular system allows flexible use and easy adaptation to specific conditions, while the raft can also serve as a resting point for swimmers. The design includes integrated lifebuoys and an emergency button with a visual beacon. A specially lowered section below the water surface significantly facilitates the rescue of a person in distress.

works.author: Jan Kroulík
works.title: MUV Modular Utility Vehicle
works.text: MUV redefines utility mobility as an adaptable infrastructural tool for future logistics and emergency operations. The autonomous symmetrical platform enables rapid integration of specialized modules. From cargo transport to tracked rescue systems. Swappable battery–pack reduce downtime and enable the use of renewable energy sources, while all-wheel drive and steering ensure agile mobility in both urban and extreme off-road conditions.

works.author: Gabriela Metzová
works.title: Rescue Robot
works.text:
pro záchranné složky a jeho hlavním účelem je lokalizovat osoby v těžko dostupných místech a navázat s nimi audiovizuální kontakt. Je vybaven systémy jako kamery s nočním viděním, termovizí, reproduktory a mikrofonem, senzory elektromagnetického pole, signálu apod. Pohyb je umožněn pomocí tzv.tendon-driven mechanismu a ohebných kloubů, které umožňují pohyb do všech směrů. 

Robot by byl poté napájen přes kabel, který mimo jiné umožňuje také stabilní přenos signálu, ale je možná i varianta na baterii.



Abstract

This robot was designed for rescue services and its main purpose is to locate people in hard-to-reach places and establish audiovisual contact with them. It is equipped with systems such as night vision cameras, thermal imaging, speakers and microphones, electromagnetic field sensors, signal sensors, etc. Movement is enabled by a tendon-driven mechanism and flexible joints that allow movement in all directions. The robot would then be powered via a cable, which also allows stable signal transmission, but a battery-powered variant is also possible.

works.author: Martin Mejvald
works.title: NIAD Filtration System
works.text: A portable water filter that combines functionality and style. It stands out with simple, rounded shapes, thanks to which it is not only aesthetic but also resistant to impacts. The unique construction allows not only filtration from common sources but is designed also for effective collection and purification of rainwater. For comfort during travel, the filter is equipped with an adjustable shoulder strap, so you have a source of drinking water always at hand, without it taking up space in a backpack.

works.author: Jakub Hrdina, Marek Ondřej, Vladimír Vykoukal
works.title: To Mars!!!
works.text:
Na Mars!!! 



MgA. Jakub Hrdina, Ph.D.

MgA. Marek Ondřej

MgA. Vladimír Vykoukal



Ateliér Průmyslový design, FMK, Univerzita Tomáše Bati ve Zlíně / 

Industrial Design Studio, FMC, Tomas Bata University in Zlín



Anotace: 

Výzkumný designérský experiment Na Mars!!! zkoumá posun disciplíny od navrhování solitérů k ucelené světotvorbě. Prostřednictvím generativních AI nástrojů při tvorbě videa vznikla komplexní audiovizuální studie marsovské infrastruktury bez lidských aktérů. Aplikovaná estetika dohledových kamer slouží jako analytický nástroj k reflexi provozu v extrémním prostředí, čímž inovuje metodologii průmyslového designu.



Annotation:

The Na Mars!!! design research experiment investigates the discipline's shift from solitary object design to comprehensive worldbuilding. Utilizing generative AI tools in video synthesis, it presents a complex audiovisual study of Martian infrastructure devoid of human actors. The applied CCTV aesthetic serves as an analytical tool to reflect on operations in extreme environments, innovating industrial design methodology.

works.author: Michaela Krčová
works.title: It’s about you
works.text: The project sees space as a state of mind. It creates places for slowing down, silence, and attention to small natural details. Instead of outside noise and constant movement, it invites people to turn their focus inward – to the present moment, personal calm, and awareness of themselves allowing a quiet pause within everyday life.

works.author: Filip Huraj
works.title: MARS53
works.text:
The Mars 53 
Annotation – Filip Huraj  
 
The Mars 53 project presents a concept of minimal housing in a colonized Mars environment in 
the year 2140. Modular dwellings in dome cities respond to extreme conditions and limited 
resources. The design addresses basic human needs, uses local materials, 3D printing, and a 
flexible interior that supports movement and long-term sustainability.  
 
 
 
Mars 53 
Anotácia – Filip Huraj  
 
 
Projekt Mars 53 predstavuje koncept minimálneho bývania v podmienkach kolonizovaného 
Marsu v roku 2140. Modulárne obydlia v kupolových mestách reagujú na extrémne prostredie a 
obmedzené zdroje. Návrh rieši primárne potreby človeka, využíva lokálne materiály, 3D tlač a 
variabilný interiér podporujúci pohyb a dlhodobú udržateľnosť.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Viktória Palúchová
works.title: Y-Domatia
works.text:
translation:

The Y-Domatia project imagines a future where a threatening celestial meteor body is reshaped by a precise laser into an upholstered chair. The object fuses chaos and control, nature and human ingenuity, turning an apocalyptic threat into a functional sculpture. In “space to space,” the energy of danger becomes an intimate place of safety—we sit on what once threatened us.

__________________________________________________________________________________________________

Plná verzia (full version):

 Slovenská verzia:

Projekt Y-Domatia vychádza z imaginárneho scenára budúcnosti, v ktorom sa k Zemi približuje masívne vesmírne teleso ohrozujúce jej existenciu. Ľudstvo reaguje zásahom technológie – extrémne presného laserového lúča, schopného na diaľku transformovať aj najtvrdšiu hmotu. Deštruktívne teleso je týmto zásahom „skrotené“ a pretvorené na funkčný objekt: kreslo. Výsledný artefakt predstavuje spojenie chaosu a kontroly, prírody a ľudskej invencie.Pôvodne apokalyptická hrozba sa mení na sochársky objekt s úžitkovou funkciou, ktorý nesie stopu svojho pôvodu aj procesu transformácie. Dielo reflektuje schopnosť človeka nielen ničiť, ale aj pretvárať práve to deštruktívne – a dávať novej forme nový význam. Čalúnené kreslo umožňuje viac možností usadenia sa. V kontexte “space to space” ide o presun energie z hrozby do intímnej zóny tela ako miesta bezpečia. 

  „prečo kreslo?“: 

  „Pretože kreslo je objekt, ktorý vyžaduje dôveru tela – sadáme si na niečo, čo nás 

 predtým ohrozovalo.“ 

English translation:

The Y-Domatia project is based on an imaginary future scenario in which a massive celestial body approaches Earth, threatening its existence. Humanity responds with the intervention of technology – an extremely precise laser beam capable of transforming even the hardest matter from a distance. The destructive body is thus “tamed” and transformed into a functional object: a chair. The resulting artifact represents a fusion of chaos and control, nature and human ingenuity. What was once an apocalyptic threat becomes a sculptural object with a utilitarian function, carrying traces of its origin and the process of transformation. The upholstered armchair allows for multiple seating positions. In the context of “space to space,” it represents a transfer of energy from a threat into the intimate zone of the body as a place of safety.

“Why a chair?” “Because a chair is an object that requires the trust of the body – we sit on something that once threatened us.”

works.author: Lujza Novosadová
works.title: Infinity loop
works.text:
Infinity loop

Autor: Lujza Novosadová

Supervisor: doc. Ing. Zuzana Tončíková, ArtD.



This project offers self-sufficient ecotourism in the Egyptian desert. Underground architecture minimizes light pollution for elite stargazing. A rooftop system ensures autonomy: collectors inspired by desert lizard gills extract air moisture for water, while integrated wind turbines produce energy. It provides an authentic, eco-conscious experience focused on desert survival and beauty.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martin Žabka
works.title: Shinrin
works.text:
Názov: ShinrinAutor: Martin Žabka	Supervisor: doc. Mgr. art. Marián Ihring, ArtD.

Filozofia:

Slovensky:

Sedenie Shinrin je inšpirované tvarovaním bonsajov a myšlienkou priniesť prírodu bližšie k človeku pracujúcemu v rušnej kancelarií. Cieľom návrhu je vytvoriť tiché a upokojujúce miesto uprostered pracovného prostredia, ktoré bude slúžiť ako únik od hluku a ruchu pracoviska. Zelená farba odkazuje na koruny stromov a podporuje kľud a sústrredenie, zatiaľ čo biela na elastických poťahoch symbolizuje čistotu a mier.



Anglicky:

	The Shinrin seating is inspired by bonsai shaping and the idea of bringing nature closer to people working in busy offices. The aim of the design is to create a quiet and calming place in the middle of the working environment that will serve as an escape from the noise and bustle of the workplace. The green color refers to tree crowns and promotes calm and concentration, while the white on the elastic covers symbolizes purity and peace.

works.author: Monika Koišová
works.title: Spojova Hub
works.text: The SPOJOVÁ HUB project focuses on the revitalization of an underutilized space behind Spojová Primary School in Banská Bystrica. The aim of the design is to create a multifunctional playground that supports movement, play, and relaxation for different age groups. The space connects after-school activities with areas for older students, forming a place for meeting, interaction, and everyday school life.

works.author: Dennis Baptist
works.title: Microcosmos
works.text:
een audiovisuele ervaring transformeren 
kaders in verbonden micro-werelden. Beelden veranderen wanneer 
een externe kracht zich tussen kaders beweegt, beïnvloedt en 
synchroniseert, en zo dynamische relaties tussen verschillende 
dimensies onthult.
